Properties.AlarmDescription: One or more PDF processing jobs failed in the last 5 minutes. Check the LOG ERRORS widget below for the stack trace.
Lambda itself crashed (unhandled exception). This is worse than a pipeline error — check for memory/timeout issues.
PDF processing is taking over 4 minutes (p95). May indicate large PDFs, slow AI responses, or network latency.

Fn::Join: {"widgets":[{"type":"text","width":24,"height":2,"x":0,"y":0,"properties":{"markdown":"# Vision Factory — PDF → JSON Pipeline\n**Function:** `vision-factory-processor`  |  **All counters show last 24 hours.**  Scroll down for live error logs from the last run."}},{"type":"metric","width":4,"height":4,"x":0,"y":2,"properties":{"view":"singleValue","title":"📄 Total Jobs (24h)","region":"

","metrics":[["AWS/Lambda","Throttles","FunctionName","vision-factory-processor",{"color":"#ff7f0e","label":"Throttles","period":86400,"stat":"Sum"}]]}},{"type":"text","width":24,"height":5,"x":0,"y":6,"properties":{"markdown":"## What each number means
| Counter | Target | What to do if wrong |
|---|---|---|
| 📄 Total Jobs | Should rise as PDFs are submitted | Low = nothing is triggering the pipeline (check your API caller or S3 event) |
| ✅ Succeeded | Should equal Total Jobs | Gap = pipeline errors. Check **LOG ERRORS** section below |
| ❌ Pipeline Errors | Should be **zero** | Any value = check log errors. Usually bad PDF, API failure, or parsing issue |
| 💥 Lambda Crashes | Should be **zero** | Means Lambda OOM, timeout, or import error — check Lambda config |
| ⏸️ Throttled | Should be **zero** | Means concurrent limit hit — raise Lambda reserved concurrency |
"}},{"type":"alarm","width":8,"height":6,"x":0,"y":11,"properties":{"title":"🚨 Active Alarms — Current Status","alarms":["

","metrics":[["AWS/Lambda","Duration","FunctionName","vision-factory-processor",{"color":"#ff7f0e","label":"p95 Duration (ms)","stat":"p95"}]],"annotations":{"horizontal":[{"value":240000,"color":"#ff7f0e","label":"4 min warning","yAxis":"left"}]},"yAxis":{"left":{"label":"ms","min":0}},"period":10800}},{"type":"metric","width":24,"height":6,"x":0,"y":17,"properties":{"view":"timeSeries","title":"✅ Successes vs ❌ Pipeline Errors (12h trend)","region":"

","metrics":[["VisionFactory/Pipeline","SuccessfulJSONOutputs",{"color":"#2ca02c","label":"Successes","stat":"Sum"}],["VisionFactory/Pipeline","PipelineErrors",{"color":"#d62728","label":"Pipeline Errors","stat":"Sum"}]],"yAxis":{"left":{"label":"Count","min":0}},"period":43200}},{"type":"text","width":24,"height":3,"x":0,"y":23,"properties":{"markdown":"## 🔍 Live Error Logs — Most Recent Errors
The table below shows the last **20 ERROR and WARNING lines** from CloudWatch Logs.  
Use this to diagnose a specific failed job. Each line is timestamped.  
> **Tip:** If you see `PipelineErrors=1` above but no entries here, the error was a Lambda crash — check Lambda Crashes counter."}},{"type":"log","width":24,"height":8,"x":0,"y":26,"properties":{"view":"table","title":"Last 20 ERROR / WARNING log lines","region":"

","query":"SOURCE '/aws/lambda/vision-factory-processor' | fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /ERROR|WARNING|error|exception|Exception|failed|Failed/
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 20"}},{"type":"text","width":24,"height":2,"x":0,"y":34,"properties":{"markdown":"## ✅ Recent Successful Completions
Lines below confirm successful pipeline runs.  
> **What to look for:** Each line has the filename and how many pages were processed."}},{"type":"log","width":24,"height":6,"x":0,"y":36,"properties":{"view":"table","title":"Last 10 successful completions","region":"

","query":"SOURCE '/aws/lambda/vision-factory-processor' | fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /SuccessfulJSONOutputs|Webhook delivered|Pipeline completed|statusCode.*200/
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 10"}}]}

BootstrapVersion.Description: Version of the CDK Bootstrap resources in this environment, automatically retrieved from SSM Parameter Store. [cdk:skip]

Assertions.AssertDescription: CDK bootstrap stack version 6 required. Please run 'cdk bootstrap' with a recent version of the CDK CLI.
